title: senecio × viscidulus
intro: Senecio × viscidulus, also known as sticky Jerusalem sage, is a hybrid of Senecio visitus and Senecio linifolius, a shrubby perennial plant native to South Africa. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and alkaloids. The health benefits of Senecio × viscidulus include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce pain and inflammation, and act as a natural antihistamine. Senecio × viscidulus is often prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, allowing its medicinal properties to be harnessed for therapeutic purposes.
